Nodes.Text: Halt, traveler. I am the warden of this village. The watch lantern is out and the nights have gotten bold. What do you need?

Choices.Text: What happened to the watch lantern?
I know a rite that can relight the lantern.
I can rekindle the lantern again.
I have the oil recipe and ward oil to reforge the lantern properly.
Let me teach you how to maintain the lantern yourself.

Nodes.Text: The ward oil ran dry three weeks back. Without it the flame won't catch — it's not ordinary oil, it's mixed with cave-moss extract and something the old warden called 'bright salt.' The lantern kept the night creatures at a distance. Now they circle closer every evening.

Choices.Text: The elder might know where to find a recipe.

Nodes.Text: You know a kindle rite? That old warden magic? If you can coax a flame from that dead wick, even for a while, the village will sleep safer tonight.
Light! Faint, but light. I can see the tree line again. It won't last — I can tell by how the flame leans — but it's more than we've had in weeks. Thank you.

Choices.Text: It's temporary. I'll find a way to make it permanent.

Nodes.Text: You have the recipe and the oil? Then let's not waste another dark night. I'll hold the housing open while you work.
That light is steady as sunrise. The creatures won't dare come close with that burning. You've given this village its nights back.

You want to teach me the old warden's craft? I've been guarding this village with a sword and a sharp eye, but I always wished I could do more than just watch the dark. If there's a way to keep this light burning, I'll learn every word.

Choices.Text: Here — this copy explains the oil mixture and wick trimming. Study it well.

Nodes.Text: Oil ratios, wick trimming, the monthly re-warding... I can do this. From now on this lantern stays lit. You've made me more than a sword-arm — you've made me a true warden.

Choices.Text: The village is in good hands.

Nodes.Text: Burning bright every night, just as you showed me. Haven't seen a creature within bowshot of the village since. The children play outside after dark again.
The usual — beasts from the wilds, bandits on the roads. Lately the creatures have been bolder. Something's stirring them up. I've doubled my patrols, but one warden can only do so much.

Choices.Text: I could help deal with some of those threats.

Nodes.Text: A willing blade is always welcome. Clear out some of the beasts near the village and you'll have our gratitude — and maybe a reward from the elder.

Choices.Text: I'll see what I can do.

Nodes.Text: Halt, traveler. I am the warden of this village. State your business.
Then pass through peacefully. We don't tolerate troublemakers here, but honest travelers are welcome. The elder can tell you more about the area if you need guidance.
This is a small settlement, but we look after our own. The elder leads us, the merchant trades goods, and I keep watch. We've had our share of trouble from the wilds, but we endure.
The usual -- beasts from the wilds, bandits on the roads. Lately the creatures have been bolder. Something's stirring them up. I've doubled my patrols, but one warden can only do so much.

Choices.Text: I could help deal with some of those threats.

Nodes.Text: A willing blade is always welcome. Clear out some of the beasts near the village and you'll have our gratitude -- and maybe a reward from the elder.

Choices.Text: I'll see what I can do.

Nodes.Text: Depends on where you are. Near the caves it's snapjaws and bats. The desert has scorpions and bandits. The jungle is full of spiders and vipers. And the old ruins... I don't like to think about what lurks in the ruins.
